perturbation.original_text: Joint Content License Relationship. Among other games, Zynga produces and distributes the ZYNGA POKER® game on a number of global platforms, including Apple iOS, Google Android, Facebook and the zynga.com website. The ZYNGA POKER® game features a Zynga Poker Tournaments Mode that Zynga can customize. Among other things, WPT is the creator of the World Poker Tour, WPT Tournaments and the WPT Invitational Tournaments. WPT Tournaments and WPT Invitational Tournaments are televised poker tournaments where a partner can promote its brand. The parties desire to work cooperatively, but independently, to use commercially reasonable efforts to engage in the marketing and promotional activities described in Exhibit A, including, but not limited to Zynga promoting the WPT brand in a WPT-branded Zynga Poker Tournament Mode, and WPT promoting the Zynga brand in WPT Tournaments and WPT Invitational Tournaments. This Agreement describes the terms of a content license and cooperative marketing relationship under which each party will independently or cooperatively engage in mutually agreed activities to promote each other's products and services throughout the Territory (as defined below).
perturbation.changed_text: Joint Content License Relationship. Among other games, Zynga produces and distributes the ZYNGA POKER® game on a number of global platforms, including Apple iOS, Google Android, Facebook and the zynga.com website. The ZYNGA POKER® game features a Zynga Poker Tournaments Mode that Zynga can customize. Among other things, WPT is the creator of the World Poker Tour, WPT Tournaments and the WPT Invitational Tournaments. WPT Tournaments and WPT Invitational Tournaments are televised poker tournaments where a partner can promote its brand. The parties desire to work cooperatively, but independently, to use commercially reasonable efforts to engage in the marketing and promotional activities described in Exhibit A, including, but not limited to Zynga promoting the WPT brand in a WPT-branded Zynga Poker Tournament Mode, and WPT promoting the Zynga brand in WPT Tournaments and WPT Invitational Tournaments. This Agreement describes the terms of a content license and cooperative marketing relationship under which each party will independently or cooperatively engage in mutually agreed activities to promote each other's products and services throughout the Designated Region (as defined below).
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Territory' to 'Designated Region' in Section 1, while 'Territory' is explicitly defined in Section 2 and Section 1.h. of the Additional Provisions, this introduces a contradiction. The agreement now refers to a 'Designated Region' without defining it, while maintaining a definition for 'Territory', creating ambiguity regarding the geographical scope of the agreement. Especially in section 2, where is defined and where the contradiction is taking place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Territory for this Agreement is worldwide, but not including Asian countries (including, but not limited to, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Brunei, Cambodia, East Timor, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Japan, Laos, Macau, Malaysia, Maldives, Mongolia, Myanmar, Nepal, North Korea, Pakistan, People's Republic of China, Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam). The parties acknowledge and agree that the rights granted hereunder by Zynga (a) with respect to the United States are granted to, held and exercised by Zynga US and (b) with respect to all other parts of the Territory are granted to, held and exercised by Zynga Ireland.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Territory' to 'Designated Region' in the initial definition section, coupled with the fact that 'Territory' is specifically defined later in the document (Section 2) while 'Designated Region' is not, creates a contradiction. Section 2 defines the 'Territory' as 'worldwide, but not including Asian countries...', but now Section 1 refers to a 'Designated Region'. Because the agreement now refers to a 'Designated Region' without defining it, while maintaining a definition for 'Territory', this creates a direct and significant conflict regarding the geographical scope of the agreement and which definition applies, therefore establishing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Royalty. Zynga will pay to WPT ten percent (10%) of the cumulative Net Revenue (as defined in Section 3.b. of the Additional Provisions) ("Royalty") from the WPT-branded Zynga Poker Tournament Mode or other such use of the WPT brand on the Zynga platform. Zynga shall not be required to pay the Royalty to the extent offset by the Annual Minimum Guarantee payments previously paid to WPT during the Term. Conversely, Zynga shall not be required to make Annual Minimum Guarantee payments to the extent offset by the Royalty previously paid to WPT during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: Compensation. Zynga will pay to WPT ten percent (10%) of the cumulative Net Revenue (as defined in Section 3.b. of the Additional Provisions) ("Royalty") from the WPT-branded Zynga Poker Tournament Mode or other such use of the WPT brand on the Zynga platform. Zynga shall not be required to pay the Compensation to the extent offset by the Annual Minimum Guarantee payments previously paid to WPT during the Term. Conversely, Zynga shall not be required to make Annual Minimum Guarantee payments to the extent offset by the Royalty previously paid to WPT during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Royalty' with 'Compensation' in the first sentence of Section 5 while leaving the rest of the section referring to 'Royalty' and Section 3 defining royalty, the agreement introduces confusion. Is 'Compensation' the same as 'Royalty'? If so, why the different name? If not, what is it? The contradicting part is right where the change happens and the following sentences.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation. Zynga will pay to WPT ten percent (10%) of the cumulative Net Revenue (as defined in Section 3.b. of the Additional Provisions) ("Royalty") from the WPT-branded Zynga Poker Tournament Mode or other such use of the WPT brand on the Zynga platform. Zynga shall not be required to pay the Compensation to the extent offset by the Annual Minimum Guarantee payments previously paid to WPT during the Term. Conversely, Zynga shall not be required to make Annual Minimum Guarantee payments to the extent offset by the Royalty previously paid to WPT during the Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Royalty' consistently, which is defined in Section 3.b. The changed text replaces 'Royalty' with 'Compensation' in the first sentence, but subsequent sentences in the same paragraph still refer to 'Royalty.' The contradicted text which is in the same location as the changed text now creates an in-text contradiction because the section now uses two different terms for the same compensation that are not properly defined or used interchangeably. The reader is left to wonder if 'Compensation' is different from 'Royalty', contradicting the clear use of 'Royalty' in the rest of the agreement. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Notices. All notices and other communications given hereunder shall be in writing and shall be sent by courier service, express mail, personal delivery or mail to the respective addresses of the parties set forth above (or at such other address as such party may designate by notice to the other party). A copy of any notice to WPT shall also be sent to WPT Enterprises, Inc., ATTN: Legal, 1920 Main Street, Suite 1150, Irvine, CA 92614. A copy of any notice to Zynga shall also be sent to Office of the General Counsel, Zynga Inc., 699 8th Street, San Francisco, CA 94103 with a copy to legalnotices@zynga.com. Notice shall be deemed given as follows: upon delivery if sent by courier service, express mail or personal delivery; and five (5) days after the date of mailing, postage prepaid, certified or registered mail if sent by mail.
perturbation.changed_text: Notifications. All notices and other communications given hereunder shall be in writing and shall be sent by courier service, express mail, personal delivery or mail to the respective addresses of the parties set forth above (or at such other address as such party may designate by notice to the other party). A copy of any Notification to WPT shall also be sent to WPT Enterprises, Inc., ATTN: Legal, 1920 Main Street, Suite 1150, Irvine, CA 92614. A copy of any Notification to Zynga shall also be sent to Office of the General Counsel, Zynga Inc., 699 8th Street, San Francisco, CA 94103 with a copy to legalnotices@zynga.com. Notice shall be deemed given as follows: upon delivery if sent by courier service, express mail or personal delivery; and five (5) days after the date of mailing, postage prepaid, certified or registered mail if sent by mail.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the section title and the initial word 'Notices' to 'Notifications' while retaining 'Notice' in the rest of the section, this creates an internal contradiction. It's unclear whether 'Notification' and 'Notice' refer to the same thing, especially concerning the legal implications of the delivery methods and timing described for 'Notice'. Also the contradicting part is right where the change happens and it keeps mentioning the word 'notice' in the rest of the paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notifications. All notices and other communications given hereunder shall be in writing and shall be sent by courier service, express mail, personal delivery or mail to the respective addresses of the parties set forth above (or at such other address as such party may designate by notice to the other party). A copy of any Notification to WPT shall also be sent to WPT Enterprises, Inc., ATTN: Legal, 1920 Main Street, Suite 1150, Irvine, CA 92614. A copy of any Notification to Zynga shall also be sent to Office of the General Counsel, Zynga Inc., 699 8th Street, San Francisco, CA 94103 with a copy to legalnotices@zynga.com. Notice shall be deemed given as follows: upon delivery if sent by courier service, express mail or personal delivery; and five (5) days after the date of mailing, postage prepaid, certified or registered mail if sent by mail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Notices' to 'Notifications' in the title and initial sentence, but the rest of the paragraph refers to 'Notice.' This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Notification' and 'Notice' refer to the same thing. The paragraph then details how 'Notice' is deemed given, creating ambiguity and a potential contradiction if 'Notification' has different requirements. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which includes using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The contradicting part is right where the change happens and it keeps mentioning the word 'notice' in the rest of the paragraph. The contradicted text is the changed text.
